name: Toptal
headline: tenX vs Toptal for AI engineering
positioning: Toptal is a broad premium freelance network. tenX is narrower: AI-verified engineers with published fees and badge-level model fluency.
aiVerification: Manual technical interview; no AI-model-specific sandbox or badge system. Verification is interviewer-dependent, not platform-enforced.
pricingModel: Toptal publishes a platform subscription and deposit, but role-specific rates and margin are not separated on the invoice.
marginNote: Third-party reviews commonly describe Toptal markup as opaque and sometimes estimate it near 50%. Treat those estimates as market commentary, not a published Toptal number.
sourceLabel: Toptal FAQ and 2026 third-party pricing reviews

name: Turing
headline: tenX vs Turing for AI engineering
positioning: Turing emphasizes global AI matching. tenX emphasizes platform-verified AI skills, NZ-first availability, and visible economics.
aiVerification: Algorithm-based matching with optional AI skill tags; no model-specific sandbox evals or public badge system.
pricingModel: Turing pricing is generally quote-based with platform margin embedded in the billed rate.
marginNote: Independent 2026 comparisons report rumours of 50-55% service margin. tenX publishes 20% contractor loading and 10% permanent placement fees.

name: Andela
headline: tenX vs Andela for AI engineering
positioning: Andela offers a large global technical network. tenX is a smaller AI-native network tuned for verified model fluency in NZ and AUS.
aiVerification: Technical assessment on intake; no published AI model eval rubric or badge system.
pricingModel: Andela uses custom quotes and monthly or hourly engagement structures that vary by seniority and scope.
marginNote: Public pricing ranges exist in market datasets, but margin visibility is limited compared with tenX published fees.
sourceLabel: Andela site and 2026 marketplace pricing summaries

name: Arc.dev
headline: tenX vs Arc.dev for AI engineering
positioning: Arc.dev is a large remote developer marketplace. tenX is more focused: model-fluent AI engineers with sandbox badges and transparent fees.
aiVerification: Profile review and skills tagging; limited AI-specific technical verification and no public model eval.
pricingModel: Arc.dev lists broad freelance ranges and commonly references percentage-based full-time hiring fees.
marginNote: Arc.dev can be fast for general remote hiring; tenX is built for AI-specific verification and published economics.
sourceLabel: Arc.dev role pages and 2026 pricing summaries

name: Lemon.io
headline: tenX vs Lemon.io for AI engineering
positioning: Lemon.io is a curated developer network. tenX is narrower and more AI-specific, with model fluency badges and published hiring margins.
aiVerification: Referral-based and manual vetting; no published model eval rubric or model-specific badge system.
pricingModel: Lemon.io contractor pricing is usually presented as an all-in hourly rate, with platform margin included rather than itemised.
marginNote: tenX publishes the platform economics directly: 20% contractor loading and 10% permanent placement fees.
